$comment: ARM64 (AArch64) target descriptor (plan 13 AS3 + plan 12 D-ML7-2.1). 100% JSON-configured — the same target-blind LIR substrate that drives x86_64 reads this file. Adding ARM64 = drop this file; no C++ change. Encoding shape: `fixed32` (32-bit fixed word + bit-field slots). Calling convention: AAPCS64 (https://github.com/ARM-software/abi-aa).

target.name: arm64
target.description: ARM64 AArch64 — minimal opcode set + AAPCS64 calling convention + fixed32 byte-encoder. Cycle-3 scope: ret (0xD65F03C0) + mov reg-reg via ORR alias (0xAA0003E0 base + Rd + Rm).

$aggregateLayoutComment: FC6 (D-FF3-1): per-ABI layout params. `natural` (scalar align = byte size, capped) is the AAPCS64 + Apple arm64 rule; maxAlignment 16 = the AArch64 max fundamental alignment (128-bit vector / __int128 / long double).
$tlsComment: TLS C2 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): the CPU's static-TLS layout convention — psABI-per-CPU data (AArch64 is Variant I under EVERY OS, so it lives on the TARGET, not the format; the x86_64 twin declares variant2). variant1 = the thread pointer (TPIDR_EL0) points AT the TCB head and the TLS block FOLLOWS it; a symbol's link-time thread-pointer offset is POSITIVE: tpoff = alignUp(tcbHeaderBytes, p_align) + templateOffset. tcbHeaderBytes = 16 — the AArch64 TCB header is two pointers (the psABI/glibc `tcbhead_t` prefix: dtv pointer + private). Consumed ONLY by the walker's tpoff helper (addTlsSymbolOffsets) when the format's tlsAccess.model is local-exec — a config-keyed formula selector, never a machine-identity branch.

opcodes.$comment: Slot 0 = the Invalid sentinel — same discipline as x86_64. Substrate's addInst guard rejects opcode 0 loudly.
Virtual-ISA pseudo-op — same role as x86's `arg`: 'this LirReg is the function's k-th input parameter'. ML7 callconv lowering rewrites to the real mov-from-arg-reg sequence.

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (FC7 C1c) — caller-side mirror of `arg`: 'this LirReg is the k-th return register of the preceding struct-returning call' (payload = per-class return ordinal). ML7 callconv lowering captures it from the return register. Declared for substrate uniformity; AAPCS64 struct returns land at C3.

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (FC7 C3, AAPCS64/Apple x8 sret) — callee-side mirror of `arg`: 'this LirReg is the incoming indirect-result (sret) pointer', read from the cc's indirectResultRegister (x8) at function entry. NO encoding: lir_callconv materializes it into `mov result, x8` (an ORR-alias), exactly like `arg`/`ret_piece`. payload unused. The caller side needs no opcode — the sret pointer is a normal prepended Call operand routed to x8 by the call payload's indirect-result bit.

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (FC12a-core, D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): the variadic register-save-area address. Declared for substrate uniformity (like read_indirect_result); AArch64's variadic ABI differs (no SysV-style register-save-area / vaListLayout this cycle), so its sysv-less CC never EMITS this op — it is materialized away by lir_callconv only for a CC that declares a vaListLayout. No encoding.

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (FC12a-core, D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): the incoming overflow-stack-args address. Declared for substrate uniformity; never emitted on a CC without a vaListLayout. No encoding (materialized away by lir_callconv).

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (D-FC12-VARIADIC-OVERFLOW-FIXED-AGGREGATE-STACK-ARGS): callee-side mirror of the caller's `ByValueStackArg` carrier — the ADDRESS of a FIXED by-value aggregate/HFA param placed WHOLLY on the incoming stack (it straddled the reg/stack boundary). lir_callconv materializes it into `lea result, [sp + totalFrameSize + callPushBytes + shadowSpaceBytes + payload]`; the PAYLOAD is this aggregate's byte offset within the incoming overflow area. DISTINCT from the va_* ops so it is NOT a va_start-detection signal (it occurs in non-variadic functions too). No encoding (materialized away).

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (c69, D-CSUBSET-ALLOCA-ADDRESS-REMATERIALIZE): a RE-REFERENCE of a body-local's stack slot — 'this fresh LirReg is the ADDRESS of local-alloca #k', k = PAYLOAD = the alloca's 0-based scan-order index. The c69 entry-block alloca-hoist makes every local's address live from entry; reusing ONE address vreg tips a register-pressure cliff, so MIR->LIR emits the `alloca` op ONCE (reserves the slot) and a fresh `lea_frame_slot k` at EACH use (tiny def->use range) — the conventional 'rematerialize frame addresses' treatment. lir_callconv materializes it into `lea result, [sp + localAreaOffset + offsetOf(k) + disp]` — byte-identical to the original alloca's `lea` (offset from the alloca-index->offset map built in scan order). An OPTIONAL single ImmInt operand is a folded CONSTANT byte displacement `disp` (a `&local[constIdx]`/`&s.field` Gep whose base IS this alloca, folded by lowerGep into ONE sp-based frame lea): cheaper (one lea) AND load-bearing — a >16 MiB `disp` in the un-folded two-lea form (`lea_frame_slot` base + `lea [base+disp]`) lowers to the arm64 MOVZ/MOVK macro `ADD Xd,base,Xd` which CLOBBERS base when regalloc coalesces the tiny-range remat base into the Gep result (base==Xd), a SIGSEGV; folding makes the base `sp` (a reserved physreg, never the result), so `MOVZ Xd;MOVK Xd;ADD Xd,sp,Xd` is safe. DISTINCT from `alloca` so it is INVISIBLE to the frame-layout slot-count scan (one slot per original alloca is preserved). On arm64 `lea` is the ADD-immediate frame-address form (the same op the alloca/va_* address pseudo-ops lower to). No encoding (materialized away). hasSideEffects=false (pure address computation, like lea/alloca); no LIR-tier pass removes/merges it.

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (c116 H1, D-WIN64-SEH-FUNCLETS): 'this fresh LirReg is the ADDRESS of local-slot #k IN THE FRAME the operand[0] register establishes' — the generic analog of llvm.localrecover. operand[0] = an establisher-frame base pointer register; PAYLOAD = k. lir_callconv materializes it into `lea result, [establisherReg + parentLocalAreaOffset(k)]` (the config FrameLayout of the frame the base establishes; on arm64 `lea` is the ADD-immediate frame-address form). Declared for substrate uniformity; x64 SEH funclets are its only consumer today (windows.json is pe-only, so no `__try` reaches arm64 and this op is never emitted here). No encoding (materialized away). hasSideEffects=false (pure address computation, like lea/alloca/lea_frame_slot).

Virtual-ISA pseudo-op (FC12b, D-FC12B-WIN64-VARIADIC-CALLEE): the Win64 HomogeneousPointer va_start base (&home[namedArgCount]). Declared for substrate uniformity; AAPCS64 uses the dual-cursor strategy (FC12c) and never emits this op. No encoding (materialized away by lir_callconv).

AAPCS64 has no native `mov reg, reg` instruction — the canonical encoding is `ORR Xd, XZR, Xm` (32-bit fixed word 0xAA0003E0 = base + Rn=XZR=31; Rd and Rm via slots). LIR's `mov` is 2-operand (result + 1 source); 3-address natively (no requires2Address).

wires.$comment: ORR Xd, XZR, Xm — bits 16..20 hold Rm (the source register's encoding). The fixed word's bits 9..5 = 0b11111 (XZR) are baked in; the wire only adds Rm bits.

variants.$comment: MOVZ Xd, #imm16 — move a 16-bit UNSIGNED immediate (zeroing the rest of the register; hw shift = 0). Fixed word 0xD2800000 (sf=1, opc=10, fixed bits 100101, hw=00). The Imm16 slot writes imm16 at bits 5..20; Rd at bits 0..4. Selected by the `imm32` operand-kind guard (the engine's immediate-operand filter — the SLOT is 16-bit, MOVZ-specific). The entry trampoline uses this to load the exit-syscall number into x8 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64). A value wider than the 16-bit slot STILL fails loud at encode (A_ImmediateOperandOutOfRange) — but the MIR->LIR const materialization now splits wide inline constants into the MOVZ + MOVK ladder BEFORE encode when the movk_lsl* family below is declared (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE, FC3.5 sweep-c3), so the encoder ceiling backstops hand-built LIR only.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64-WIDE-IMMEDIATE): MOVK Xd, #imm16, LSL #16 — keep-other-bits 16-bit insert at bits 31:16. ARM ARM C6.2.227: sf [31] | opc=11 [30:29] | 100101 [28:23] | hw [22:21] | imm16 [20:5] | Rd [4:0]; X-base 0xF2800000, hw=01 -> 0xF2A00000. The MIR->LIR const materialization emits the MINIMAL ladder: MOVZ #chunk0 (zeroing all 64 bits) + one MOVK per NONZERO higher 16-bit chunk — selected by constant magnitude at the LOWERING (an operand's VALUE cannot guard encoding variants), capability-probed by mnemonic presence (x86's imm32 mov form never consults this family). requires2Address: the ISA reads Xd's other bits — the LIR shape is result==operand[0] (the accumulating register), wire index 0 lands in Rd (the 2-addr dest-wire satisfies the result-placement validate rule). ONE deliberately width-ABSENT X-form variant: the ladder is seeded by MOVZ (which zeroes bits 63:32), so the X-MOVK is value-correct for width-32 constants too (chunks 2/3 are never emitted at width 32) — the W-ORR zext / xor_rdx_zero width-invariance precedent.

MOVK Xd, #imm16, LSL #32 — hw=10 -> 0xF2C00000 (X-only: sf=0 reserves hw>=10). Chunk 2 of the wide-immediate ladder; emitted only for width-64 constants whose bits 47:32 are nonzero (sign-extended negative int32 patterns).
MOVK Xd, #imm16, LSL #48 — hw=11 -> 0xF2E00000 (X-only). Chunk 3 of the wide-immediate ladder.
AAPCS64 RET (X30 implicit) = 0xD65F03C0. No operands at LIR level; the link register x30 is implicit per the calling convention.
Hard-trap. AArch64 BRK #0 = 0xD4200000. Cycle-3 includes for completeness so a synthesized LIR can terminate without depending on `ret` semantics.

ARM64 binary ops (AS3b, all 3-address natively — no requires2Address). Fixed-word base patterns: ADD reg-reg = 0x8B000000; SUB = 0xCB000000; AND = 0x8A000000; ORR = 0xAA000000; EOR = 0xCA000000. Wires: Rd at 0..4 (dest), Rn at 5..9 (src1), Rm at 16..20 (src2).

encoding.$comment: FC3 c2 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS): the reg-reg shape is width-keyed 64/32 — the W-form is the SAME shifted-register ADD word with sf (bit 31) CLEARED (ARM ARM C6.2.5: a W-register write zeroes the upper 32 bits of the X register, the C-defined unsigned-int wraparound for free). The imm12 shape stays width:64-only — its sole producer is the callconv sp-adjust (Ptr-width); a width-32 add-imm inst would match NOTHING and fail loud rather than silently compute with a 64-bit carry.

variants.$comment: ADD Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x0B000000 (the X-form 0x8B000000 with sf=0).

ADD Xd, Xn, #imm12 (immediate, single word). Fixed word 0x91000000 (sf=1, op=0 add, S=0, sh=0). imm12 at bits 10..21 via the Imm12 slot; Rd 0..4, Rn 5..9. Used by the callconv prologue/epilogue stack adjust (`add sp, sp, #frame`) when the frame fits 0..4095. 2-operand LIR form [reg, imm]: operand 0 → Rn (also the dest via resultSlot), operand 1 → Imm12. `immMax:4095` routes ONLY a frame ≤4095 here; a larger value falls through to the 2-word shifted variant below (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12).

ADD Xd, Xn, #imm (shifted imm12 word-pair, D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12). For a frame in (4095, 0xFFFFFF] this emits TWO words: word0 = `ADD Xd, Xn, #(V & 0xFFF)` (0x91000000, sh=0), word1 = `ADD Xd, Xd, #((V>>12) & 0xFFF), LSL #12` (0x91400000 = 0x91000000|0x400000, sh=1). SCRATCH-FREE: word1 reads its OWN dest (Xd) as the source base — extraResultSlots thread the result reg into word1.Rd AND word1.Rn. The single imm12.hilo24 wire (word0) carries the VALUE; the encoder splits it and writes lo→word0 / hi→word1, both into the imm12 bit-window (10..21). `immMin:4096`/`immMax:16777215` route ONLY a >4095, ≤16MiB frame here; >16MiB stays fail-loud (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB).

ADD sp, sp, #imm (MOVZ/MOVK + EXTENDED-register 3-word form, D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB). The epilogue mirror of SUB's 3-word form. word0 = `MOVZ x16, #(V & 0xFFFF)` (0xD2800010), word1 = `MOVK x16, #((V>>16) & 0xFFFF), LSL #16` (0xF2A00010), word2 = `ADD sp, sp, x16` (the EXTENDED-register form 0x8B3063FF: option=011 UXTX, bit 21 set, Rd=Rn=sp=31, Rm=x16=16). x16=IP0 scratch baked; NO resultSlot (sp=31 baked into word2 — rule-G baked-dest exemption). Same immMin/immMax routing + >2GiB fail-loud as SUB.
FC3 c2: SUB Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x4B000000 (the X-form 0xCB000000 with sf=0).

SUB Xd, Xn, #imm12 (immediate, single word). Fixed word 0xD1000000 (sf=1, op=1 sub, S=0, sh=0). imm12 at bits 10..21; Rd 0..4, Rn 5..9. Used by the callconv prologue stack adjust (`sub sp, sp, #frame`) when the frame fits 0..4095. Same 2-operand [reg, imm] LIR form as ADD-immediate. FC3 c2: width:64-only like add's imm12 shape (sole producer is the Ptr-width sp-adjust; a width-32 sub-imm fails loud). `immMax:4095` routes ONLY a ≤4095 frame here; larger falls through to the 2-word shifted variant (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12).

SUB Xd, Xn, #imm (shifted imm12 word-pair, D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12). For a frame in (4095, 0xFFFFFF] this emits TWO words: word0 = `SUB Xd, Xn, #(V & 0xFFF)` (0xD1000000, sh=0), word1 = `SUB Xd, Xd, #((V>>12) & 0xFFF), LSL #12` (0xD1400000 = 0xD1000000|0x400000, sh=1). word1 reads its OWN dest (Xd) — SCRATCH-FREE via extraResultSlots. Single imm12.hilo24 wire (word0) carries the VALUE; the encoder splits lo→word0 / hi→word1. The prologue's `sub sp,sp,#frame` becomes `sub sp,sp,#lo; sub sp,sp,#hi,LSL#12` — the transient sp-lo misalignment between the two words is invisible (no access/call between them; V is 16-aligned). `immMin:4096`/`immMax:16777215`; >16MiB fail-loud.

SUB sp, sp, #imm (MOVZ/MOVK + EXTENDED-register 3-word form, D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB). For a frame in (0xFFFFFF, 0x7FFFFFFF] (>16 MiB, ≤2 GiB) this emits THREE words: word0 = `MOVZ x16, #(V & 0xFFFF)` (0xD2800010, Rd=x16=16 baked), word1 = `MOVK x16, #((V>>16) & 0xFFFF), LSL #16` (0xF2A00010, hw=01, Rd=x16), word2 = `SUB sp, sp, x16` (the EXTENDED-register form 0xCB3063FF: option=011 UXTX, bit 21 set, Rd=Rn=sp=31, Rm=x16=16 — NOT the shifted-register 0xCB000000 where #31=XZR → `sp±0` corrupt frame). x16 is AAPCS64 IP0, the architecturally-blessed intra-procedure scratch, free at the prologue/epilogue; it is BAKED into all three base words (NO resultSlot — the destination sp=31 is baked into word2, validate's rule-G `imm32.movzmovk` baked-dest exemption). The single imm32.movzmovk wire (operand 1, word0) carries the VALUE; the encoder splits lo16→word0 / hi16→word1. Operand 0 (sp) wires to word2's `rn` (re-confirming the baked sp=31). `immMin:16777216`/`immMax:2147483647` route ONLY a >16MiB, ≤2GiB frame here; a frame > 2 GiB flows through int32 as NEGATIVE → magnitude nullopt → no variant matches → fail-loud (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-2GIB).

opcodes.$comment: D-CSUBSET-VLA (C1b): `sub_sp_reg sp, <sizeReg>` — subtract a RUNTIME byte count (in a register) from the stack pointer (the dynamic-alloca core, `int a[n]` => `sub sp, alignUp(n*4,16)`). ★ THE XZR-VS-SP TRAP: a shifted-register SUB (0xCB000000) encodes reg 31 as XZR, so `sub sp, sp, Xm` there would compute `sp - 0` and SILENTLY discard the size. The CORRECT form is the EXTENDED-register SUB (option=011 UXTX, bit 21 set), where reg 31 in Rd/Rn IS the stack pointer: fixedWord 0xCB2063FF (= the 0xCB3063FF precedent baked into the >16MiB sp-adjust macro, with Rm CLEARED to 0). Rd=Rn=sp=31 are BAKED; operand1(size) wires into Rm (bits 16..20). operand0(sp) wires into Rn (bits 5..9) — sp's hwEncoding IS 31, so this is IDEMPOTENT with the baked Rn=31 (the per-position wire check requires every operand wired). Sanity: 0xCB2063FF | (16<<16) == 0xCB3063FF (the `SUB sp,sp,x16` word). result:none (SP mutates in place); hasSideEffects=true so no pass drops/reorders it vs the sp_copy that captures the moved SP. Emitted by MIR->LIR with the physical stack-pointer operand; callconv passes it through.

D-CSUBSET-VLA (C1b): `sp_copy dst, src` = `add dst, src, #0` (the ADD-IMMEDIATE form, fixedWord 0x91000000, imm12=0 baked) — used for the three frame-pointer plumbing roles a VLA function needs: prologue FP<-SP, epilogue SP<-FP, and the VLA-base capture base<-SP. ★ Like sub_sp_reg this uses the SP-capable form: the add-immediate encoding treats reg 31 as the STACK POINTER (not XZR), so `add sp, x29, #0` / `add x29, sp, #0` / `add base, sp, #0` all move the real SP — a register-register ORR (`mov`, where reg 31 = XZR) would read/write XZR instead. resultSlot rd = dst; operand0=src -> rn. A DISTINCT mnemonic from `add`/`mov` so it can be hasSideEffects=true (reads/writes the physical SP; no pass may drop or reorder it across the sub_sp_reg).

D-CSUBSET-VLA (C5): `sp_restore sp, <savedReg>` = `mov sp, savedReg` = `add sp, savedReg, #0` (the ADD-IMMEDIATE form, fixedWord 0x9100001F: Rd=sp=31 baked, imm12=0) — RESTORE the stack pointer to a previously-captured watermark (a StackSave's `sp_copy`), the block-scope VLA teardown emitted on every NON-return exit of a VLA-declaring scope. ★ Like sub_sp_reg/sp_copy this uses the SP-capable ADD-immediate encoding (reg 31 = the STACK POINTER, NOT XZR): `add sp, saved, #0` moves the real SP, where a register-register ORR `mov` (reg 31 = XZR) would write XZR. Rd=sp=31 is BAKED; operand0(sp) wires into rd (IDEMPOTENT with the baked 31 — sp's hwEncoding IS 31, mirroring sub_sp_reg's idempotent operand0→rn), operand1(saved) wires into Rn (bits 5..9, the source). result:none (SP mutates in place — NOT an SSA value; NEVER the sp_copy-with-SP-as-result shape, audit fix #5). A DISTINCT mnemonic so it stays hasSideEffects=true (reads/writes the physical SP; no pass may drop or reorder it across the sub_sp_reg).
MUL Xd, Xn, Xm = MADD Xd, Xn, Xm, XZR. Base = 0x9B000000 | (XZR=31 << 10) = 0x9B007C00. Slots: Rd, Rn, Rm (Ra=XZR baked into fixedWord).

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: MUL Wd, Wn, Wm = MADD Wd, Wn, Wm, WZR — 0x1B007C00 (the X-form 0x9B007C00 with sf=0; the W product IS the C unsigned-int product mod 2^32).

opcodes.$comment: c103 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-UMULH): UMULH Xd, Xn, Xm — the high 64 bits of the unsigned 64x64 128-bit product. fixedWord 0x9BC07C00: data-processing (3-source) with op31=110 (bits 23:21 — UMULH; SMULH=010 → 0x9B407C00, MADD/MUL=000 → 0x9B007C00) + Ra=XZR(11111) baked in, o0=0, sf=1 (audit-recomputed vs the ARM ARM). The MIR->LIR Rule-1 NATIVE realization of MIR UMulH: a result-bearing 3-address op, no implicit registers, no pre-op (contrast x86's mov-rax + umul_op core). UMULH is inherently 64-bit (no W-form); the u64 `__umulh` is the only consumer, so a single width-64 variant.

FC17.9(b) (D-CSUBSET-BITCOUNT-INTRINSICS): CLZ Xd, Xn — count leading zeros (data-processing 1-source). fixedWord 0xDAC01000 (X, sf=1 = 3670020096) / 0x5AC01000 (W, sf=0 = 1522536448); opcode field 000100 (RBIT=000000 below; REV16/32=00000x/00001x). Rn (bits 9:5) = the source operand (wire 0), Rd (bits 4:0) = the result. arm64 CLZ is DEFINED at 0 = the width (32/64) — EXACTLY the MIR Clz contract — so it is Clz's NATIVE realization (the DSS `clz` verb, shared with x86 LZCNT). Also the 2nd half of arm64's ctz: RBIT then CLZ. A 1-source op like `neg`/`not` but the operand wires to `rn` (data-proc-1-source) not `rm`.

FC17.9(b) (D-CSUBSET-BITCOUNT-INTRINSICS): RBIT Xd, Xn — reverse the bit order (data-processing 1-source, opcode field 000000). fixedWord 0xDAC00000 (X = 3670016000) / 0x5AC00000 (W = 1522532352). Rn (bits 9:5) = source (wire 0), Rd (bits 4:0) = result. Used ONLY by arm64's ctz realization: ctz(x) = CLZ(RBIT(x)) — reversing turns the lowest set bit into the highest, so leading-zeros of the reversed value = trailing-zeros of the original (defined at 0 = width via CLZ(0)=width). x86 declares no `rbit` (it has direct TZCNT).

c104 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-ATOMIC-CAS): LDAXR Wt/Xt, [Xn] — load-acquire EXCLUSIVE (arms the exclusive monitor; the acquire half of the LL/SC CAS loop). W-form 0x885FFC00 = size=10 | 001000 | o2=0 L=1 o1=0 | Rs=11111 | o0=1 | Rt2=11111 | Rn | Rt; X-form flips size to 11 -> 0xC85FFC00. Result = the loaded value (Rt, bits 4:0 = the `rd` slot); operand 0 = the address base (Rn). The MIR AtomicCas value width picks W (the Win32 LONG i32 CAS) vs X.

c104 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-ATOMIC-CAS): STLXR Ws, Wt/Xt, [Xn] — store-release EXCLUSIVE (succeeds iff the monitor armed by LDAXR is still held; the release half of the LL/SC loop). W-form 0x8800FC00 = size=10 | 001000 | o2=0 L=0 o1=0 | Rs | o0=1 | Rt2=11111 | Rn | Rt; X-form 0xC800FC00. Result = the STATUS register Ws (0=stored, 1=monitor lost -> retry) — it lives in the Rs bit-field (20:16), which is the generic `rm` slot position, so resultSlot:'rm' (the slot vocabulary is bit-positional, not role-named). Operand 0 = the stored value (Rt, bits 4:0 = the `rd` slot position — an operand MAY wire to rd, the MOVK dest-wire precedent); operand 1 = the address base (Rn). The value width picks W vs X; the status is ALWAYS a W write (zero-extending — the loop's `cmp status, #0` runs at the default 64-bit width on the strength of that zero-extension).

FC17.9(d) atomic Phase C (D-CSUBSET-ATOMIC): LDAR Wt/Xt, [Xn] — load-ACQUIRE (RCsc), the acquire/seq_cst realization of MIR AtomicLoad. Derived from LDAXR by SETTING o2 (bit 23): LDAR = LDAXR | (1<<23). W-form 0x88DFFC00 (= 0x885FFC00 | 0x800000), X-form 0xC8DFFC00. VERIFIED by aarch64-as: `ldar w0,[x0]`=88dffc00, `ldar x0,[x0]`=c8dffc00. LDAR has NO offset field (addresses [Xn], disp 0), so the UNIFIED [ptr, MemBase, MemOffset] shape (which x86's mov-load needs) wires ptr->Rn (bits 5..9), result->Rd (bits 0..4 = Rt, the loaded value), MemBase->membase.noscale + MemOffset->memoffset.zero (width-0 markers; memoffset.zero FAILS LOUD on a nonzero disp — a naturally-aligned atomic is always offset 0, the base+index-lea precedent). Value width picks W (i32 / _Atomic int) vs X (i64 / pointer).

FC17.9(d) atomic Phase C (D-CSUBSET-ATOMIC): STLR Wt/Xt, [Xn] — store-RELEASE (RCsc), the release/acq_rel realization of MIR AtomicStore. Derived from STLXR by SETTING o2 (bit 23) AND baking Rs=11111 (STLR has NO status output, unlike STLXR whose Rs is the status reg): STLR = 0x8800FC00 | (1<<23) | (0x1F<<16) = 0x889FFC00 (W), 0xC89FFC00 (X). VERIFIED by aarch64-as: `stlr w0,[x0]`=889ffc00, `stlr x0,[x0]`=c89ffc00. result:none (no status reg). UNIFIED [value, ptr, MemBase, MemOffset] shape (the STUR store precedent): value->Rd (bits 0..4 = Rt data reg), ptr->Rn (bits 5..9), MemBase->membase.noscale + MemOffset->memoffset.zero (fail-loud nonzero disp). Value width picks W vs X.

FC17.9(d) atomic Phase C (D-CSUBSET-ATOMIC): STLR again — the SEQ_CST realization of MIR AtomicStore. LDAR/STLR are RCsc, so a release store IS a seq_cst store on arm64 (NO DMB) — BOTH the store_release and store_seqcst slots bind the IDENTICAL STLR encoding (the x86 twin diverges: store_release=mov, store_seqcst=xchg). Same words/wiring as store_release above (W 0x889FFC00, X 0xC89FFC00). Declared so the pure-slot-presence probe finds a seq_cst-store realization on arm64 (never fail-loud on a legal seq_cst store).

FC1 V2-4.X (2026-06-10): NEG Xd, Xm = SUB Xd, XZR, Xm (shifted-register alias, Rn=XZR baked). Base = 0xCB000000 | (XZR=31 << 5) = 0xCB0003E0. Slots: Rd, Rm. Surfaced by the modulo corpus' negative literals — under the BASELINE (no-ConstFold) pipeline a unary minus reaches LIR as a live `neg`, and arm64 had never declared one (every prior arm64 corpus avoided unary minus).

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: NEG Wd, Wm = SUB Wd, WZR, Wm — 0x4B0003E0 (the X-form 0xCB0003E0 with sf=0).

opcodes.$comment: Cluster-F F2 (core_bitwise): bitwise NOT (one's complement) — MVN Xd, Xm = ORN Xd, XZR, Xm (logical shifted-register alias, Rn=XZR baked, like NEG=SUB Xd,XZR,Xm above). arm64 had NO `not` opcode (the MIR Not op's LIR mnemonic), so source-level `~` failed L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing while x86 had it. ORN = the ORR family (opc=01) with the N bit (bit 21) set; base = 0xAA2003E0 (sf=1 | opc=01 | 01010 | shift=0 | N=1 | Rm=00000[wire] | imm6=0 | Rn=11111[XZR] | Rd=00000[result]). 3-address native (no requires2Address — Rd and Rm differ, like neg/sdiv). Slots: Rd (result), Rm (wire 0).

variants.$comment: MVN Wd, Wm = ORN Wd, WZR, Wm — 0x2A2003E0 (the X-form 0xAA2003E0 with sf=0).

opcodes.$comment: FC1 V2-4.X (2026-06-10): SDIV Xd, Xn, Xm — data-processing (2 source), sf=1. Base = 0x9AC00C00 (sf=1 | S=0 | 11010110 | Rm[20:16] | opcode 000011[15:10] | Rn[9:5] | Rd[4:0]). The MIR→LIR div lowering's Rule-1 NATIVE realization: result-bearing 3-address op, no implicit registers, no pre-op (contrast x86 cqo+idiv_op). ARM64 SDIV never traps — divide-by-zero yields 0 and INT_MIN/-1 wraps (A-profile), so the C-UB divide cases are non-trapping on this target. SMod has no hardware instruction: the lowering realizes rem = n - (n/d)*d via sdiv + the fused `msub` (D-LIR-MOD-MSUB-FUSION ✅ closed FC3.5 sweep-c3; the generic mul+sub expansion remains the fallback for targets without a fused op).

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: SDIV Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x1AC00C00 (the X-form 0x9AC00C00 with sf=0; ARM ARM C6.2.250 data-processing 2-source).

opcodes.$comment: FC1 V2-4.X (2026-06-10): UDIV Xd, Xn, Xm — base = 0x9AC00800 (opcode field 000010 vs SDIV's 000011). Unsigned divide; same Rule-1 native shape as sdiv. UMod expands via udiv+mul+sub.

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: UDIV Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x1AC00800 (the X-form 0x9AC00800 with sf=0).

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-LIR-MOD-MSUB-FUSION): MSUB Xd, Xn, Xm, Xa = Xa − Xn·Xm — data-processing (3 source), ARM ARM C6.2.230: sf [31] | 00 [30:29] | 11011 [28:24] | 000 [23:21] | Rm [20:16] | o0=1 sub [15] | Ra [14:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0]. X-base = 0x9B000000 | (1<<15) = 0x9B008000 (the MADD base the `mul` alias builds on, with o0=1). The MIR→LIR rule-3 remainder expansion PREFERS this fused form when declared: msub r, q, d, n = n − q·d in ONE instruction (operand 0 = quotient → rn, operand 1 = divisor → rm, operand 2 = dividend → ra), replacing the generic mul+sub pair. First consumer of the fixed32 `ra` slot (bits 10..14).

variants.$comment: MSUB Wd, Wn, Wm, Wa — 0x1B008000 (the X-form 0x9B008000 with sf=0; the W result IS the C 32-bit remainder mod 2^32).

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c1 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS shifts residue): LSLV Xd, Xn, Xm — logical shift left, variable. Data-processing (2 source) like sdiv/udiv: sf [31] | 0 0 [30:29] | 11010110 [28:21] | Rm [20:16] | opcode 001000 LSLV [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C6.2.215) = base 0x9AC02000. NATIVE 3-address — no implicit registers, no count pin (contrast x86's CL contract); the MIR->LIR shift lowering's native arm emits ONE op. The hardware masks the count: Xm mod 64 (X-form) / Wm mod 32 (W-form, sf=0 = 0x1AC02000) — C declares counts >= width UB, so no masking is emitted. CONSTANT counts also ride this form (the count materializes through the const mov like any operand); the LSL-immediate alias (UBFM) is a peephole-class refinement, deliberately NOT declared this cycle.

variants.$comment: LSLV Wd, Wn, Wm — 0x1AC02000 (sf=0; count mod 32).

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c1: LSRV Xd, Xn, Xm — LOGICAL shift right, variable (MIR LShr; zero-fill, unsigned operands). Same data-processing (2 source) shape; opcode field 001001 = base 0x9AC02400 (ARM ARM C6.2.217). W-form 0x1AC02400.

FC3.5 sweep-c1: ASRV Xd, Xn, Xm — ARITHMETIC shift right, variable (MIR AShr; sign-fill, signed operands). Opcode field 001010 = base 0x9AC02800 (ARM ARM C6.2.21). W-form 0x1AC02800. DSS defines C's implementation-defined negative >> as sign-fill on every target — same contract as x86 SAR.
ORR Xd, Xn, Xm = 0xAA000000 base (sf=1, opc=01, fixed pattern). NOT the same as the mov-alias which bakes Rn=XZR into the fixed word — this is the general-purpose 3-address OR.

D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64 (ARM64 control-flow): materialize a 0/1 GPR from the NZCV flags by condition — the AArch64 analog of x86 `setcc`. ARM64 has no `setcc`; the canonical idiom is `CSET Xd, <cond>` = `CSINC Xd, XZR, XZR, invert(cond)` (when the INVERTED condition is FALSE — i.e. the requested condition is TRUE — the false-arm increments XZR→1; otherwise 0). Single fixed word base 0x9A9F07E0 (2594113504): sf=1, CSINC opcode, Rm=XZR=31<<16, Rn=XZR=31<<5, op2=01, Rd at bits 0..4. The cond nibble is placed at bits 12..15 (condBitPos=12) and INVERTED (condInvert=true) from the inst payload via condCodeFromPayload + the schema's condCodeEncoding table. LIR shape mirrors x86 setcc: result=value, ZERO value operands (reads FLAGS), cond from payload. lowerICmp emits `cmp` then `setcc(cond)` then `zext`. VERIFY: `cset x0, gt` = GT(0xC) inverted to 0xD at bits 12..15 = 0x9A9FD7E0.

Zero-extend a 32-bit value to 64-bit — AArch64 `MOV Wd, Wm` = `ORR Wd, WZR, Wm` (a 32-bit register op auto-zeroes the upper 32 bits of the X register, so a W-form ORR IS the W→X zero-extend). Single fixed word base 0x2A0003E0 (704644064): sf=0 (32-bit), ORR opc=01, Rn=WZR=31<<5, Rd at bits 0..4, Rm (source) at bits 16..20 via the one reg wire. Mirrors x86_64's `zext` LIR shape: result=value, ONE reg operand. The natural consumer is lowerICmp's setcc→zext pair (a clean 0/1 r64); also reused by MIR ZExt. FC3.5 sweep-c1 (D-CSUBSET-ZEXT-32-TO-64) + D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS: the variants are now WIDTH-KEYED (the byte/half forms below forced the split off match-any, per the convergence rule). This mov word (0x2A0003E0) realizes BOTH Bool (width 64) and U32 (width 32) — each zero-extends via the W-write (bits 63:32 cleared). The sub-native U16/U8 sources do NOT use the mov: a promoted U16/U8 register carries STALE upper bits the mov would copy, so they MASK via UBFM (UXTH 0x53003C00 / UXTB 0x53001C00). VERIFY: zext of w1→x0 via the mov form (Rd=0, Rm=1) = 0x2A0103E0.

variants.$comment: width 64 (Bool default, flags=0): MOV Wd, Wm = ORR Wd, WZR, Wm (0x2A0003E0). A Bool 0/1 register zero-extends to X via the W-write (bits 63:32 cleared). Width-keyed now that the byte/half forms below are declared (the convergence rule forbids a match-any sibling beside width-keyed ones).
width 32 (U32 source): the SAME MOV Wd, Wm (0x2A0003E0) — a full U32 in a W register zero-extends to X (the W-write zeroes 63:32). This IS the U32->U64 conversion.

width 16 (D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS): UXTH Wd, Wn = UBFM Wd, Wn, #0, #15 (0x53003C00) — zero-extend the low HALF-WORD (the x86_64 movzx r64,r/m16 peer). MASKS to the low 16 bits, so a promoted U16 register's stale bits 31:16 are cleared (UNLIKE the width-32 mov, which would copy them). UBFM source is Rn (not Rm). Selected when the ZExt source type is U16 (width 16).

width 8 (D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS): UXTB Wd, Wn = UBFM Wd, Wn, #0, #7 (0x53001C00) — zero-extend the low BYTE (the x86_64 movzx r64,r/m8 peer). MASKS to the low 8 bits. UBFM source is Rn (not Rm). Selected when the ZExt source type is U8 (width 8).

opcodes.$comment: FC3 c2 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS): NEW opcode — sign-extend a 32-bit value to 64-bit. AArch64 `SXTW Xd, Wn` = the SBFM alias `SBFM Xd, Xn, #0, #31`: word 0x93407C00 = sf=1 [31] | opc=00 [30:29] | 100110 [28:23] | N=1 [22] | immr=000000 [21:16] | imms=011111 [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C6.2.263 SXTW / C6.2.234 SBFM). Mirrors x86_64's `sext` (movsxd r64, r/m32) LIR shape: result=value, ONE reg operand on the Rn slot. The MIR→LIR SExt gate admits I32 sources only — exactly this form's inherent width. First consumer: the LP64 `int` → `unsigned long` UAC conversion in the datamodel_runtime_width corpus.

variants.$comment: width 32: SXTW Xd, Wn (SBFM Xd,Xn,#0,#31 = 0x93407C00) — I32→I64. Width-keyed (was width-absent) now that the byte form below is declared — convergence-fix D forbids mixing a match-any sibling with a width-keyed one. The MIR→LIR SExt arm threads widthFlagsForType(source): an I32 source → width 32 → here.

width 8 (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN): SXTB Xd, Wn = SBFM Xd, Xn, #0, #7 — sign-extend the low BYTE to 64 bits (the x86_64 `movsx r64,r/m8` peer). Word 0x93401C00 = the SXTW word with imms 31→7 (bits 15:10): sf=1 | opc=00 | 100110 | N=1 | immr=0 | imms=7 | Rn | Rd (ARM ARM C6.2.* SBFM/SXTB). Selected when the SExt source type is Char (width 8 on the LIR inst) — the char→int widening. Reads exactly the low byte (the char), so the source register's upper bits (zero-extended by the LDURB byte load) are irrelevant.

width 16 (D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS): SXTH Xd, Wn = SBFM Xd, Xn, #0, #15 — sign-extend the low HALF-WORD to 64 bits (the x86_64 movsx r64,r/m16 peer). Word 0x93403C00 = the SXTW word with imms 31->15 (bits 15:10): sf=1 | opc=00 | 100110 | N=1 | immr=000000 | imms=001111 | Rn | Rd. Selected when the SExt source type is I16 (width 16) — the short->int widening. Reads exactly the low half-word, so the source register's upper bits are irrelevant.

opcodes.$comment: FC3 c2 (D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS): NEW opcode — truncate a 64-bit value to 32 bits. AArch64 `MOV Wd, Wm` = `ORR Wd, WZR, Wm` (0x2A0003E0 — sf=0, Rn=WZR baked; the SAME word the `zext` opcode uses, because the ISA-level operation is identical: a W-register write zeroes the upper 32 bits). Two mnemonics deliberately exist over one word — each conversion mnemonic carries its INHERENT width pair (trunc: 64→32 mod-2^32, C's conversion semantics; zext: Bool/byte→64) and the MIR→LIR gates police each independently. The variant is WIDTH-KEYED 32 like x86's trunc: a Trunc to 16/8 bits reaching the assembler matches NOTHING and fails loud.

Allocate a stack-frame slot (virtual / pseudo-op — NO encoding, exactly like x86_64's `alloca`). Payload = spill-slot/local index threaded verbatim from MIR Alloca. Result is a pointer-class virtreg holding the slot's effective address. The ML7 callconv pass (lir_callconv) materializes this into a frame-relative `lea result, [sp + offset]` once the frame layout is known — target-blind, and arm64 already has `lea` (ADRP+ADD), so no encoder change is needed; this declaration just lets MIR Alloca lower on arm64. A function with body locals (`int n = 42;`) emits one alloca per local.

Direct AND indirect call, one mnemonic with per-callee-kind variants (FC4 c2). Direct call to a symbol — AArch64 `BL imm26` (branch-with-link); indirect call through a register — AArch64 `BLR Xn` (branch-with-link-to-register). Declared under the engine's GENERIC `call` verb (NOT the ISA spellings 'bl'/'blr'), the same convention every other opcode in this file already follows (`mov`=ORR-alias, `add`=ADD, `xor`=EOR, `or`=ORR): the target's mnemonics ARE the engine's verb vocabulary; the real ISA mnemonics live in this comment. This is what lets the format-blind trampoline emitter (`opcodeByMnemonic("call")`) and the MIR→LIR `MnemonicSlot::Call` resolve the call on ANY target — and keeping BLR a VARIANT (not a second opcode) keeps isCall / functionHasCalls / caller-saved-clobber semantics shared by construction. BL fixed-word base = 0x94000000 (opcode bits 26..31 = 0b100101); imm26 wire at bits 0..25 emits a call26 relocation, bits left at 0 (linker patches at link time). BLR fixed-word base = 0xD63F0000 with the callee register in the Rn slot (bits 5..9): BLR X9 = 0xD63F0120.

variants.$comment: FC4 c2: BLR Xn — 0xD63F0000 | Rn<<5 (indirect call through a register). fixedWord 3594452992 = 0xD63F0000; sanity precedent: the shipped ret row's 0xD65F03C0 = 3596551104 (RET X30, same D6 family).

opcodes.$comment: AArch64 SVC #0 = 0xD4000001 — supervisor call (the syscall instruction). Zero-operand at LIR level; the Linux AArch64 syscall ABI puts the syscall number in x8 (the format's processExit.syscallNumGpr), args in x0..x7, return in x0. Mirrors x86_64's `syscall` (0F 05). Added at D-LK10-ENTRY-ARM64 so the entry trampoline can issue the exit_group syscall and terminate the process. fixedWord 3556769793 = 0xD4000001 (SVC with imm16=0).

Spill-reload pseudo-op (target-blind, NO encoding — exactly like x86_64's). Payload = spill-slot index. The ML7 callconv pass materializes this into the target's real `load` (the LDUR opcode below) ONLY when a function actually spills / restores a callee-saved reg; the spill-rewrite pass requires the mnemonic to EXIST upfront. A register-pressure-light leaf (e.g. `return 42`) never emits one.

Spill-store pseudo-op (target-blind, NO encoding). Payload = spill-slot index. Operand[0] = the physical reg being stored. Materialized into the target's real `store` (the STUR opcode below) by the ML7 callconv pass, only when a function spills / saves a callee-saved reg.

D-AS-REGALLOC-WIDE-CALL-OPERAND-COUNT (option E): outgoing-stack-arg store carrier (target-blind, NO encoding). Emitted by the PRE-REGALLOC wide-call lowering pass for every outgoing call argument beyond the cc's register-passed capacity (config: argGprs/argFprs = 8 GPR / 8 FPR on AAPCS64). Operand[0] = the arg VALUE vreg (regalloc assigns it; its live range ends HERE, not at the call). Payload = the arg's OVERFLOW SLOT INDEX (monotonic, source-order NSAA cursor). lir_callconv materializes it into `store operand, [sp + shadowSpaceBytes + payload*outgoingSlotSize]` (shadowSpaceBytes=0 on AAPCS64) via the STUR/scaled-STR chokepoint — byte-identical to the pre-existing placement-loop store. hasSideEffects=true so no pass reorders it off its call.

Memory LOAD — AArch64 LDUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (unscaled, signed 9-bit byte offset). Fixed word 0xF8400000 (size=11 64-bit, opc=01 load, unscaled mode bits 11..10 = 00). Operand layout (shared LIR load form): [base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)] + result. Wires: result→Rt (rd, bits 0..4), base_reg(index 0)→Rn (bits 5..9), MemOffset(index 2)→Imm9 (bits 12..20, signed). The MemBase(index 1) carries no AArch64 bits (scale must be 1; the walker validates it). The callconv materializes frame_load into this. Offset range -256..255; a wider frame fails loud (scaled LDR imm12 = future).

variants.$comment: width 64: LDUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (0xF8400000). Width-keyed (was width-absent) now that the byte form below is declared — convergence-fix D forbids mixing a match-any sibling with a width-keyed one. Every GPR integer/pointer load carries width 64 (memAccessWidthFlags returns 0 ⇒ width 64 for non-Char GPR types).

width 8 (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN): LDURB Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0x38400000 — the LDUR word with size 11→00). Loads ONE byte, ZERO-extending it into Wt (and a W-write zeroes bits 63:32 of Xt) — a char/byte memory access MUST be byte-EXACT, a 64-bit LDUR of a 1-byte string/array element reads 7 bytes past it. ZERO-extend is the signedness-AGNOSTIC byte fetch (the x86_64 movzx peer); sign semantics live in the following SXTB cast (char→int), which re-reads the low byte. Same operand layout as the 64-bit form. Selected when the Load's result type is Char (width 8 via memAccessWidthFlags).

width 32 (D-LIR-INT-MEMORY-WIDTH-EXACT): LDUR Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0xB8400000 — the LDUR word with size 11→10). Loads 4 bytes into Wt; a W-write zeroes bits 63:32 of Xt, so the int lands clean. A 4-byte int load MUST be width-exact — a 64-bit LDUR of a PACKED int element reads 4 bytes past it. Same operand layout as the 64-bit form. Selected when the Load's result type is a 4-byte integer (I32/U32, or `long` under LLP64) — width 32 via memAccessWidthFlags.

width 16 (D-LIR-INT-MEMORY-WIDTH-EXACT): LDURH Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0x78400000 — the LDUR word with size 11→01). Loads 2 bytes, ZERO-extending into Wt (signedness-agnostic, the LDURB peer); the short→int promote (SXTH/UXTH = D-CSUBSET-SUBNATIVE-ALU-FORMS, not yet encoded ⇒ short VALUE use fails loud) re-reads the low half-word. Same operand layout. Selected when the Load's result type is I16/U16 — width 16 via memAccessWidthFlags.

opcodes.$comment: Memory STORE — AArch64 STUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (unscaled). Fixed word 0xF8000000 (opc=00 store). Operand layout (shared LIR store form): [value_reg, base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)], no result. Wires: value_reg(index 0)→Rt (rd, bits 0..4), base_reg(index 1)→Rn (bits 5..9), MemOffset(index 3)→Imm9 (bits 12..20, signed). MemBase(index 2) carries no bits (scale must be 1). The callconv materializes frame_store into this.

variants.$comment: width 64: STUR Xt, [Xn, #simm9] (0xF8000000). Width-keyed (was width-absent) now that the byte form below is declared — convergence-fix D forbids mixing a match-any sibling with a width-keyed one. Every GPR integer/pointer store carries width 64 (memAccessWidthFlags returns 0 ⇒ width 64 for non-Char GPR types).

width 8 (D-CSUBSET-CHAR-STRING-VALUE-CODEGEN): STURB Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0x38000000 — the STUR word with size 11→00). Writes EXACTLY the low byte of Wt — a 64-bit STUR of a 1-byte element clobbers 7 neighbours. Signedness-agnostic (only the low 8 bits are written). Same operand layout as the 64-bit form. Selected when the stored value's type is Char (width 8 via memAccessWidthFlags).

width 32 (D-LIR-INT-MEMORY-WIDTH-EXACT): STUR Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0xB8000000 — the STUR word with size 11→10). Writes EXACTLY 4 bytes — a 64-bit STUR of a PACKED int element clobbers 4 bytes of the neighbour / overruns the frame slot (the array-storage crash this cycle fixes). Same operand layout as the 64-bit form. Selected when the stored value's type is a 4-byte integer (I32/U32, or `long` under LLP64) — width 32 via memAccessWidthFlags.

width 16 (D-LIR-INT-MEMORY-WIDTH-EXACT): STURH Wt, [Xn, #simm9] (0x78000000 — the STUR word with size 11→01). Writes EXACTLY 2 bytes (the low half-word of Wt) — a wider store of a packed short clobbers its neighbour. Signedness-agnostic. Same operand layout. Selected when the stored value's type is I16/U16 — width 16 via memAccessWidthFlags.

opcodes.$comment: D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12 — Memory LOAD, unsigned-offset form: AArch64 LDR Xt, [Xn, #pimm] (scaled, unsigned 12-bit byte offset). The large-frame sibling of `load` (LDUR, signed imm9): a frame offset BEYOND the unscaled ±256 reach takes this form. Fixed word 0xF9400000 (= the LDUR base | (1<<24), the unsigned-offset mode bit). SAME operand layout as `load` ([base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)] + result) so the callconv emits the SAME shape — only the opcode (mnemonic) differs, picked at lowering time from the offset value (the variant selector matches operand KINDS, not the value). Wires: result→Rt (rd, bits 0..4), base_reg→Rn (bits 5..9), MemOffset→Imm12Scaled (bits 10..21, the SCALED field imm12=byteOffset/accessSize). MemBase carries no bits (scale==1). Offset must be non-negative + access-size-aligned + scale ≤4095 (reach 32760 for 64-bit); else fail-loud (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12).

variants.$comment: width 64: LDR Xt, [Xn, #pimm] (0xF9400000). accessSize 8 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset/8 (e.g. [sp,#192] ⇒ imm12=24). Every GPR integer/pointer load carries width 64.
width 8: LDRB Wt, [Xn, #pimm] (0x39400000 — the unsigned-offset word with size 11→00). accessSize 1 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset (unscaled in effect). Selected when the Load's result type is Char (width 8).
width 32: LDR Wt, [Xn, #pimm] (0xB9400000 — size 11→10). accessSize 4 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset/4 (e.g. [x3,#64] ⇒ imm12=16). Selected for a 4-byte integer load (I32/U32, or long under LLP64) — width 32.
width 16: LDRH Wt, [Xn, #pimm] (0x79400000 — size 11→01). accessSize 2 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset/2. Selected for I16/U16 — width 16.

opcodes.$comment: D-ASM-AARCH64-LARGE-FRAME-IMM12 — Memory STORE, unsigned-offset form: AArch64 STR Xt, [Xn, #pimm] (scaled). The large-frame sibling of `store` (STUR, signed imm9). Fixed word 0xF9000000 (= the STUR base | (1<<24)). SAME operand layout as `store` ([value_reg, base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)], no result). Wires: value_reg→Rt (rd, 0..4), base_reg→Rn (5..9), MemOffset→Imm12Scaled (10..21, scaled). MemBase carries no bits. Offset non-negative + access-size-aligned + scale ≤4095, else fail-loud.

variants.$comment: width 64: STR Xt, [Xn, #pimm] (0xF9000000). accessSize 8 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset/8. Every GPR integer/pointer store carries width 64.
width 8: STRB Wt, [Xn, #pimm] (0x39000000 — size 11→00). accessSize 1 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset. Selected when the stored value's type is Char (width 8).
width 32: STR Wt, [Xn, #pimm] (0xB9000000 — size 11→10). accessSize 4 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset/4. Selected for a 4-byte integer store — width 32.
width 16: STRH Wt, [Xn, #pimm] (0x79000000 — size 11→01). accessSize 2 ⇒ imm12 = byteOffset/2. Selected for I16/U16 — width 16.

opcodes.$comment: TLS C2 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): materialize the current thread's TLS base pointer (tp) into a GPR — the AArch64 form is `MRS Xd, TPIDR_EL0`, ONE self-contained fixed word 0xD53BD040 (3577466944 = MRS with op0=3 op1=3 CRn=13 CRm=0 op2=2, the EL0 software thread-ID register) | Rd at bits 0..4 (the standard `rd` slot — zero new slot vocabulary). ZERO-OPERAND shape, deliberately DIFFERENT from x86's 1-op `[memoffset]` row: the system-register read consumes NO memory operand and NO prefix byte, so the lowering's capability probe (lowerThreadLocalGlobalAddr) sees the `[]` variant and emits a bare `tlsbase %tp` — the format tlsAccess block's segmentPrefixByte/baseDisplacement are x86-template machinery this shape never reads (the arm64-ELF JSON declares them 0, inert). Pure read (no side effects); never a terminator. The paired tpoff ADD/ADD macro is the `lea` [reg, symbol] TLS variant below.

Load-effective-address — AArch64 ADRP+ADD pair (D-AS4-3 multi-instruction macro-op materializing a module symbol's address into Xd). Mirrors x86_64's 1-operand RIP-relative `lea reg, [rip+sym]` so `lowerGlobalAddr` is target-blind: ONE SymbolRef operand wired into BOTH words. word0 = ADRP Xd, sym@PG (0x90000000=2415919104): Rd at bits 0..4; immlo[30:29]+immhi[23:5] left ZERO — the adr_prel_pg_hi21 reloc patches the ±4 GiB page delta. word1 = ADD Xd, Xd, #:lo12:sym (0x91000000=2432696320, the ADD-imm base shared with `add`'s imm variant): Rd at 0..4 AND Rn at 5..9 (BOTH = the result reg via extraResultSlots — ADD reads its own dest as the source base); imm12[21:10] left ZERO — the add_abs_lo12_nc reloc patches. Both relocs target the SAME symbol at byte offsets base+0 and base+4. The two `sym.patch` wires write NO immediate bits (the linker owns them); they carry only relocationKind + wordIndex.

variants.$comment: 2-op block-address form — ADRP+ADD of a SYNTHETIC per-block symbol (D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO `&&label`). Byte-IDENTICAL to the 1-op `[symbol]` ADRP+ADD form above (same fixedWords, same Rd/Rn extraResultSlots, same two sym.patch wires emitting adr_prel_pg_hi21 on word0 + add_abs_lo12_nc on word1 against the SymbolRef at operand 0). The trailing BlockRef (operand 1) is METADATA, wired to NOTHING — it names the target LIR block so the assembler can bind the synthetic symbol to that block's byte offset (recorded as a BlockSymPatch; the linker assigns the interior-block VA). Distinct guard `[symbol, blockref]` so the variant matcher routes this 2-operand `lea` here instead of failing A_NoMatchingEncodingVariant.

FRAME-RELATIVE lea (single word) — `lea Xd, [base + #disp]` = AArch64 `ADD Xd, Xn, #imm12`. This is the form the ML7 callconv's `emitFrameAddr` materializes from `alloca` (a body-local's effective address): 3-op no-index shape [base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)] + result, MIRRORING x86_64's 3-op `lea` (so the callconv stays target-blind — it emits the same operand layout for both ISAs). Single fixed word 0x91000000 (2432696320, the ADD-imm base, shared with `add`'s imm variant + the SymbolRef lea's word1): base_reg→Rn (bits 5..9), result→Rd (bits 0..4), MemOffset→Imm12 (UNSIGNED, bits 10..21; frame offsets are non-negative). MemBase carries no AArch64 bits (scale must be 1; the walker validates it via membase.noscale). `immMax:4095` routes ONLY a frame offset ≤4095 here; a larger offset falls through to the 2-word shifted variant below (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12).

FRAME-RELATIVE lea (shifted imm12 word-pair, D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-IMM12) — `lea Xd, [base + #disp]` with disp in (4095, 0xFFFFFF]. The GEP's array-element address (e.g. `int big[9000]; big[8999]` at sp+35996) under a >4095-byte frame. Emits TWO words reusing ADD's base: word0 = `ADD Xd, Xn, #(disp & 0xFFF)` (0x91000000, sh=0), word1 = `ADD Xd, Xd, #((disp>>12) & 0xFFF), LSL #12` (0x91400000, sh=1). SCRATCH-FREE: word0 reads the base (Xn) into Xd, word1 reads Xd→Xd (extraResultSlots thread the result reg into word1.Rd AND word1.Rn). The MemOffset (operand 2) is the VALUE-bearing operand the matcher keys on; its single imm12.hilo24 wire (word0) carries the displacement, split lo→word0 / hi→word1 by the encoder. MemBase (operand 1, scale==1) carries no bits (membase.noscale, word0). `immMin:4096`/`immMax:16777215`; a >16MiB offset stays fail-loud (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB). width:64 (a frame address is Ptr-width); the disjoint imm-range makes it unambiguous against the single-word variant above regardless of width.

FRAME-RELATIVE lea (MOVZ/MOVK + EXTENDED-register 3-word form, D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-16MIB) — `lea Xd, [sp + #disp]` with disp in (0xFFFFFF, 0x7FFFFFFF] (>16 MiB, ≤2 GiB). The GEP's high-element address (e.g. `int big[5000000]; big[4999999]` at sp+~20MB) under a >16MiB frame. Emits THREE words, SCRATCH-FREE: the displacement materializes into the lea's DEST reg Xd, then an extended-register ADD off sp. word0 = `MOVZ Xd, #(disp & 0xFFFF)` (0xD2800000, Rd=Xd via resultSlot), word1 = `MOVK Xd, #((disp>>16) & 0xFFFF), LSL #16` (0xF2A00000, Rd=Xd via extraResultSlots word1.rd), word2 = `ADD Xd, sp, Xd` (the EXTENDED-register form 0x8B2063E0: option=011 UXTX, bit 21 set, Rn=sp=31 baked, Rd=Xd + Rm=Xd via extraResultSlots word2.rd/word2.rm). The dest reg threads through ALL THREE words (resultSlot=rd word0 + three extraResultSlots) — no separate scratch needed. The MemOffset (operand 2) is the VALUE-bearing operand the matcher keys on; its single imm32.movzmovk wire (word0) carries the displacement, split lo16→word0 / hi16→word1 by the encoder. Operand 0 (base=sp) → word2's `rn` (re-confirming the baked sp=31); operand 1 (membase, scale==1) → membase.noscale (word2, no bits). `immMin:16777216`/`immMax:2147483647`; a >2GiB offset goes negative through int32 → fail-loud (D-ASM-AARCH64-FRAME-OFFSET-BEYOND-2GIB).

NEGATIVE-DISP lea (single word, D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB) — `lea Xd, [base + #disp]` with disp in [-4095, -1] = AArch64 `SUB Xd, Xn, #|disp|`. The `p[-1]`/`z[-1]` char-buffer lookback (a NEGATIVE const-disp GEP) that x86 encodes in one signed-disp32 `lea` but arm64's UNSIGNED imm12 ADD form could not — c94 gives it a NATIVE single-instruction SUB (replacing c93's materialize-into-a-fresh-GPR + base+index fold, which cost 5-7 instructions incl. a dead const). The negative MIRROR of the positive `ADD Xd,Xn,#imm12` variant above, op-bit SET: single fixed word 0xD1000000 (3506438144 = the ADD-imm base 0x91000000 | 0x40000000, sf=1 op=1 sub S=0 sh=0), shared with `sub`'s imm12 variant. base_reg→Rn (5..9), result→Rd (0..4), MemOffset→Imm12 (bits 10..21 — the encoder writes |disp| there; the SUB base makes it a subtract). MemBase carries no bits (scale must be 1, membase.noscale). `negMemoffset:true` routes ONLY a NEGATIVE memoffset here (the sign matcher); `immMax:4095` bounds |disp|≤4095 (a larger magnitude falls through to the shifted / MOVZ-MOVK negative variants below). SUB imm encoding verified vs ARM ARM C6.2.* (add/subtract immediate: sf|op|S|100010|sh|imm12|Rn|Rd) + aarch64-gcc objdump.

NEGATIVE-DISP lea (shifted imm12 word-pair, D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB) — `lea Xd, [base + #disp]` with |disp| in (4095, 0xFFFFFF]. The negative mirror of the positive shifted-imm12 ADD word-pair, op-bit SET: word0 = `SUB Xd, Xn, #(|disp| & 0xFFF)` (0xD1000000, sh=0), word1 = `SUB Xd, Xd, #((|disp|>>12) & 0xFFF), LSL #12` (0xD1400000 = 0xD1000000|0x400000, sh=1). SCRATCH-FREE: word1 reads its OWN dest (Xd) — extraResultSlots thread the result reg into word1.Rd AND word1.Rn. The MemOffset (operand 2) is the sign-matched value operand; its single imm12.hilo24 wire (word0) carries |disp|, split lo→word0 / hi→word1 by the encoder. `negMemoffset:true` + `immMin:4096`/`immMax:16777215` (|disp| magnitude); a >16MiB magnitude falls through to the MOVZ/MOVK form below. Fixed words = the `sub` opcode's shifted word-pair.

NEGATIVE-DISP lea (MOVZ/MOVK + EXTENDED-register 3-word form, D-AS4-ARM64-NEGATIVE-DISP-LEA-NATIVE-SUB) — `lea Xd, [base + #disp]` with |disp| in (0xFFFFFF, 0x7FFFFFFF]. The negative mirror of the positive MOVZ/MOVK 3-word form, op-bit SET on the operation word. SCRATCH-FREE: |disp| materializes into the lea's DEST reg Xd, then an EXTENDED-register SUB off the base. word0 = `MOVZ Xd, #(|disp| & 0xFFFF)` (0xD2800000, Rd=Xd via resultSlot), word1 = `MOVK Xd, #((|disp|>>16) & 0xFFFF), LSL #16` (0xF2A00000, Rd=Xd via extraResultSlots word1.rd), word2 = `SUB Xd, Xn, Xd` (the EXTENDED-register form 0xCB206000: sf=1 op=1 sub, option=011 UXTX bits 15:13, bit 21 set, Rn=base wired 5..9, Rd=Xd + Rm=Xd via extraResultSlots word2.rd/word2.rm). Unlike the POSITIVE 3-word form (which bakes Rn=sp=31), the NEGATIVE form WIRES Rn to the base register (operand 0, word2) — a derived-pointer `p[-N]` base, not sp. The dest reg threads through all three words. MemOffset (operand 2) sign-matched; its imm32.movzmovk wire (word0) carries |disp|, split lo16→word0 / hi16→word1. `negMemoffset:true` + `immMin:16777216`/`immMax:2147483647`; a magnitude > 2 GiB flows through int32 as a value whose |x| is unrepresentable → no variant → fail-loud (the residual, mirrors the positive >2GiB gap). SUB-extended base 0xCB206000 = the positive lea word2 0x8B2063E0 minus the baked Rn=sp (0x3E0) with the ADD op-bit flipped to SUB (0x8B→0xCB), verified by bit-decode.

BASE+INDEX lea (D-AS4-ARM64-BASE-INDEX-LEA) — `lea Xd, [base + index*1 + 0]` = AArch64 `ADD Xd, Xn, Xm` (shifted-register add, LSL #0). The 4-op form `lowerGep` emits for an indexed pointer access `p[n]` ([base_reg, index_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp=0)]), MIRRORING x86_64's 4-op SIB lea so the GEP stays target-blind (closing the gap that made `p[n]` fail loud on arm64 for int AND char alike). The GEP emits scale=1 + disp=0 ALWAYS today, so this single ADD is the complete realization: base→Rn (5..9), index→Rm (16..20), result→Rd (0..4); MemBase→membase.noscale (scale MUST be 1, fail-loud) and MemOffset→memoffset.zero (disp MUST be 0 — the register-add form has NO displacement field, and bits 10..21 overlap Rm so an Imm12 wiring would corrupt it; fail-loud on nonzero). Fixed word 0x8B000000 (the ADD reg-reg base, shared with `add`'s reg-reg variant). A scale!=1 (typed-array Gep scaling by sizeof(T)) needs the shifted form `ADD Xd,Xn,Xm,LSL #log2(scale)` — a FUTURE consumer (D-AS4-ARM64-INDEXED-LEA-SCALE, co-design with ML6 address-mode synthesis), NOT built speculatively.

TLS C2 (D-CSUBSET-THREAD-LOCAL): 2-op thread-pointer-offset form — `lea Xd, [tp + tpoff(sym)]` = the AArch64 local-exec ADD/ADD hi12/lo12 word-pair (gcc -ftls-model=local-exec parity): word0 = `ADD Xd, Xn(tp), #hi12, LSL #12` (0x91400000 = ADD-imm sh=1, imm12 field ZERO — reloc tls-tprel-hi12 patches (S+A)>>12 in, range-checked [0, 0xFFFFFF]), word1 = `ADD Xd, Xd, #lo12` (0x91000000 = ADD-imm sh=0, imm12 ZERO — reloc tls-tprel-lo12 patches (S+A)&0xFFF in, magnitude-free). S = the POSITIVE Variant-I tpoff the walker bit-cast into symbolVa (addTlsSymbolOffsets). SCRATCH-FREE via the SAME extraResultSlots threading the ADRP+ADD [symbol] variant above uses: word1 reads its own dest (Xd → word1.Rd AND word1.Rn). Operand 0 (the `tlsbase`-produced tp reg) → word0.Rn; operand 1 (the SymbolRef) emits BOTH relocations through the generic sym.patch marker — at word offsets 0 and 4 of the macro, both against the same symbol. Distinct guard [reg, symbol] — no other lea variant carries that shape. The x86 twin is the [reg, symbol] memreloc.disp32 lea in x86_64.target.json; the LIR the lowering emits is IDENTICAL (tlsbase reg + lea [reg, sym]) — only the declared encodings differ.

opcodes.$comment: D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26 (ARM64 conditional control-flow): integer compare writing the NZCV flags, no GPR result. AArch64 `CMP Xn, Xm` is an alias for `SUBS XZR, Xn, Xm` (subtract-and-set-flags discarding the result into the zero register). Fixed word 0xEB00001F = SUBS (sf=1, op=1, S=1) with Rd=31 (XZR) baked in; Rn at bits 5..9, Rm at bits 16..20. Mirrors x86's `cmp` LIR shape (InvalidLirReg result, 2 reg operands) — ML5 lowerICmp + lowerCondBr emit it before the conditional branch. NO resultSlot (the flags are the implicit output). Reuses the Rn/Rm slots already exercised by the add/sub reg-reg variants.

variants.$comment: FC3 c2: CMP Wn, Wm = SUBS WZR, Wn, Wm — 0x6B00001F (the X-form 0xEB00001F with sf=0). A U32 compare MUST read 32 bits — a bitcast-from-negative operand carries sign-extended upper bits the X-form would misread.

FC3.5 sweep-c1: CMP Xn, #imm12 = SUBS XZR, Xn, #imm12 — add/subtract (immediate), ARM ARM C6.2.395: sf=1 [31] | op=1 sub [30] | S=1 [29] | 100010 [28:23] | sh=0 [22] | imm12 [21:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd=XZR=31 [4:0] = 0xF100001F. FIRST consumer: the MIR->LIR non-fused CondBr arm's `cmp cond, #0` over a PHI-produced Bool (a short-circuit `&&`/`||` result used as an if/while condition cannot fuse with its compare). x86's cmp had its reg-imm form from the start; this arm64 gap was masked until the S2 marker fix let logical-op conditions reach codegen. Reuses the Imm12 slot (bits 10..21) the callconv's sub-sp frame adjust already exercises; the walker range-checks 0..4095 fail-loud.
FC3.5 sweep-c1: CMP Wn, #imm12 — the X-form with sf=0: 0x7100001F. The 32-bit sibling per the FC3-c2 width axis (a U32-typed Bool-ish compare reads 32 bits).

opcodes.$comment: D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26: unconditional intra-function jump. AArch64 `B <label>` (encoding 0x14000000, opcode bits 26..31 = 0b000101). The 26-bit imm field (bits 0..25, SCALED by 4) is the SIGNED word displacement to the target block — resolved at ASSEMBLE time via the Imm26 slot + per-function patch list (NOT a linker relocation; the same Imm26 slot is symbol-bearing for the BL/`call` form, but a BlockRef operand here selects the intra-function path). Operand[0] is a BlockRef naming the target basic block. Mirrors x86's `jmp` (E9 rel32). Imm26's ±128 MiB reach covers any single function.

D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO: indirect branch `BR Xn` — 0xD61F0000 | Rn<<5 (unconditional branch to a register, NO link/return-address — the BLR sibling captures one). fixedWord 3592355840 = 0xD61F0000; mirrors the BLR row (0xD63F0000, same D6 family — BR clears bit 21). Rn at bits 5..9 via the `rn` slot. The MIR IndirectBr lowers to this; the target-address register is materialized by a preceding adrp+add `lea` of the target block's synthetic symbol (BlockAddress). terminatorKind indirect-br → variadic address-taken successors.

D-AS3-BLOCK-REL-IMM19/26: conditional intra-function jump with mandatory fallthrough. 2-WORD macro: `B.cond <ifTrue>` (word 0) then `B <ifFalse>` (word 1) — the AArch64 analog of x86's `0F 8x rel32; E9 rel32` compound. word0 = B.cond base 0x54000000 (opcode bits 24..31 = 0b01010100, bit 4 = 0); the cond nibble (bits 0..3) comes from the inst's payload (TargetCondCode) via `condCodeFromPayload` + the schema's `condCodeEncoding` table; the 19-bit signed PC-relative offset (bits 5..23, scaled by 4) goes to the Imm19 slot → ifTrue block. word1 = B base 0x14000000 with the Imm26 slot → ifFalse (fallthrough) block. Both displacements resolve at assemble time (BlockRef operands, no relocation). operand[0]=ifTrue (taken when cond holds), operand[1]=ifFalse. A future optimizer elides word1 when ifFalse is the next-laid-out block (D-OPT-JCC-FALLTHROUGH). Mirrors the x86 jcc wiring + the lea multi-word fixedWords+wordIndex shape.

D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FADD Dd, Dn, Dm — scalar double, FP data-processing (2 source). Base 0x1E602800 = 0 0 0 11110 [31:24] | ftype=01 double [23:22] | 1 [21] | Rm [20:16] | opcode 001010 FADD [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C7.2.*). 3-operand NATIVE like sdiv — deliberately NO requires2Address (x86's fadd carries it for ADDSD's 2-address shape; declaring it here would force a pointless extra fmov through the legalize pass). FC3.5 sweep-c2 (D-CSUBSET-F32-CODEGEN): the F32 sibling FADD Sd, Sn, Sm (ftype=00 → base 0x1E202800) rides the width axis — D-form keyed 64, S-form keyed 32.

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FADD Sd, Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E202800.

opcodes.$comment: Cluster-F F3 (float_mul_sub): FMUL Dd, Dn, Dm — scalar double, FP data-processing (2 source). The [15:10] field = {opcode[3:0], 10}; FMUL opcode=0000 -> 000010, so base = 0x1E600800 (= fadd 0x1E602800 with the opcode bits 0010->0000, i.e. clear bit 13). F32 sibling FMUL Sd,Sn,Sm = ftype=00 -> 0x1E200800 (D-form minus the ftype bit 0x400000). 3-operand NATIVE (no requires2Address, like fadd/fdiv); commutative. Mirrors fadd's wires (Rn=op0, Rm=op1).

variants.$comment: FMUL Sd, Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E200800.

opcodes.$comment: Cluster-F F3 (float_mul_sub): FSUB Dd, Dn, Dm = Dn - Dm — scalar double, FP data-processing (2 source). FSUB opcode=0011 -> [15:10]=001110, base = 0x1E603800 (= fadd 0x1E602800 | 0x1000, opcode 0010->0011). F32 sibling = 0x1E203800. NON-COMMUTATIVE: op[0]=Rn (minuend), op[1]=Rm (subtrahend), Rd = Rn - Rm = a - b. 3-operand NATIVE (no requires2Address). Mirrors fadd's wire shape so the minuend/subtrahend order is preserved.

variants.$comment: FSUB Sd, Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E203800.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: FDIV Dd, Dn, Dm — scalar double divide, FP data-processing (2 source), opcode 000110 at [15:10]: base 0x1E601800 (ftype=01); F32 sibling FDIV Sd, Sn, Sm = 0x1E201800 (ftype=00) — ARM ARM C7.2 FDIV (scalar). 3-operand NATIVE (no requires2Address). First consumer: NaN construction (0.0/0.0) for the D-COND-FLOAT-NAN-TRUTHINESS-FCMP corpus witnesses; closes the fdiv stub on this target.

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FDIV Sd, Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E201800.

opcodes.$comment: c78 (D-CSUBSET-FLOAT-NEG-ENCODING): FNEG Dd, Dn — scalar double floating-point NEGATE, FP data-processing (1 source). arm64 has a NATIVE fp-negate (unlike x86, which has no fneg instruction and takes the sign-mask XORPD fallback in mir_to_lir — the two realizations diverge by CAPABILITY, probed via this opcode's declared-encoding presence). IEEE-exact by construction: FNEG flips ONLY the sign bit (bit 63), so -(+0.0)=-0.0, -(-0.0)=+0.0, and -NaN preserves the payload while flipping the sign — the signed-zero + NaN-sign semantics C requires. Base 0x1E614000 = 000 11110 [31:24] | ftype=01 double [23:22] | 1 [21] | opcode=000010 FNEG [20:15] | 10000 [14:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C7.2 FNEG scalar; opcode 000001 would be FABS, 000000 FMOV). 1-source NATIVE — Rd (result), Rn (wire 0 = the value negated). NO requires2Address (Rd and Rn are independent, like neg/not). F32 sibling FNEG Sd, Sn = ftype=00 -> base 0x1E214000 rides the width axis (D-form keyed 64, S-form keyed 32).

variants.$comment: FNEG Sd, Sn — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E214000 = 505495552.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2 (FCmp LIR lowering): FCMP Dn, Dm — floating-point compare writing NZCV, no register result (the float sibling of `cmp`). Base 0x1E602000 = 000 11110 [31:24] | ftype=01 [23:22] | 1 [21] | Rm [20:16] | op=00 [15:14] | 1000 [13:10] | Rn [9:5] | opcode2=00000 [4:0] (ARM ARM C7.2 FCMP; opcode2 bit4=1 would be FCMPE, the signaling form — DSS does not model the FP environment, so the quiet FCMP serves all predicates; the NZCV comparison RESULTS are identical). F32 sibling FCMP Sn, Sm = 0x1E202000 (ftype=00). NZCV outcomes: equal → Z=1(C=1); less → N=1; greater → C=1; unordered → C=1,V=1 — the truth table the float condCodeEncoding arms below realize (full derivation at mir_to_lir floatCmpPlan).

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FCMP Sn, Sm — ftype=00 single, base 0x1E202000.

opcodes.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: float↔float precision change for MIR FPTrunc/FPExt — FCVT (scalar): 000 11110 [31:24] | type [23:22] = SOURCE type | 1 [21] | 0001 [20:17] | opc [16:15] = DEST type | 10000 [14:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (ARM ARM C7.2 FCVT). The width axis keys on the SOURCE (the MIR→LIR FPTrunc/FPExt arm threads a widthOverride from the source operand's type — the result-width default would select exactly the WRONG direction): F32 source → FCVT Dd, Sn (type=00, opc=01 → 0x1E22C000, the FPExt widen); F64 source → FCVT Sd, Dn (type=01, opc=00 → 0x1E624000, the FPTrunc narrow).

variants.$comment: F64 source → FCVT Sd, Dn (narrow to single).
F32 source → FCVT Dd, Sn (widen to double).

opcodes.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FCVTZS Xd, Dn — double to signed 64-bit integer, round toward zero (FP→integer conversion). Base 0x9E780000 = sf=1 [31] | 00 | 11110 [28:24] | ftype=01 double [23:22] | 1 [21] | rmode=11 toward-zero [20:19] | opcode=000 FCVTZS [18:16] | 000000 [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0]. The result is GPR-class via the MIR integer type (regClassFor) — rd carries the X destination, the rn wire the D source; the 5-bit slots are class-blind hwEncodings, exactly the GPR-dest/FPR-src split x86's cvttsd2si makes through modrm.reg/modrm.rm. MIR FPToSI is the producer. FC3.5 sweep-c2: the F32-source sibling FCVTZS Xd, Sn (ftype=00 → 0x9E380000) rides the SOURCE-width axis (the FPToSI arm threads the source's width — the X destination is unchanged).

variants.$comment: FC3.5: FCVTZS Xd, Sn — ftype=00 single source, base 0x9E380000.

opcodes.$comment: c92 (D-CSUBSET-FP-TO-UI-CODEGEN, the c78 arm64 residual): FCVTZU — float to UNSIGNED integer, round toward zero. The EXACT MIRROR of fp_to_si above: same 'Conversion between FP and integer' group, same field layout (sf [31] | 00 | 11110 [28:24] | ftype [23:22] | 1 [21] | rmode=11 toward-zero [20:19] | opcode [18:16] | 000000 [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0]), opcode=001 FCVTZU where FCVTZS is 000 (+0x00010000). Same SOURCE-float-width axis (the mir_to_lir FPToUI arm threads the source's width, c78) and the same FIXED X destination as fp_to_si — a U32 result reads the low 32 bits of the Xd result, VALUE-CORRECT for any double/float in [0, 2^32) (the arm64 analog of x86 fp_to_ui's CVTTSD2SI r64 low-32 convention; gcc's `fcvtzu w0, d0` agrees on every in-range value, out-of-range is C 6.3.1.4p1 UB). ★ UNLIKE x86 (whose signed CVTTSD2SI needs the deferred >2^63 conditional-subtract, D-CSUBSET-UI-FROM-FP-UNSIGNED-I64), FCVTZU Xd covers the FULL unsigned-i64 range NATIVELY — that anchor is x86-ONLY; the arm64 leg has NO >2^63 residual (gcc emits the identical `fcvtzu x0, d0`). MIR FPToUI is the producer. FCVTZU Xd,Dn = 0x9E790000 = FCVTZS Xd,Dn[0x9E780000] | opcode bit [0x00010000]; FCVTZU Xd,Sn = 0x9E390000 = FCVTZS Xd,Sn[0x9E380000] | 0x00010000 (both cross-checked against the ARM ARM field decode above + aarch64-gcc objdump).

variants.$comment: F64 source → FCVTZU Xd, Dn — ftype=01 double, base 0x9E790000 (the low 32 bits are the in-range U32 result; the full Xd is the NATIVE full-range U64).
F32 source → FCVTZU Xd, Sn — ftype=00 single, base 0x9E390000.

opcodes.$comment: D-CSUBSET-INT-FLOAT-CONVERSION (int→float): SCVTF — Scalar Convert Signed integer to Floating-point. The REVERSE of FCVTZS above. 'Conversion between FP and integer' group: sf [31] | 00 [30:29] | 11110 [28:24] | ftype [23:22] | 1 [21] | rmode=00 [20:19] | opcode=010 SCVTF [18:16] | 000000 [15:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0]. The result is FPR-class (regClassFor sees the F64 MIR result) — rd carries the D destination, the rn wire the X/W integer source; the 5-bit slots are class-blind hwEncodings, exactly the GPR-src/FPR-dest split x86's cvtsi2sd makes through modrm.rm/modrm.reg. MIR SIToFP is the producer. The width axis keys on the SOURCE INTEGER width (the mir_to_lir SIToFP arm threads the source's int width): a 64-bit source -> SCVTF Dd, Xn (sf=1, width=64), a 32-bit source -> SCVTF Dd, Wn (sf=0, width=32). UNLIKE x86's single REX.W bit, arm64 sf+ftype are INDEPENDENT, so BOTH source-int widths keep ftype=01 (the F64 DESTINATION, Dd) — `Dd,Wn` is the i32->f64 form, the arm64 analog of cvtsi2sd xmm,r32. DESIGN NOTE (audit + qemu-arm64 scrutiny — verify these fixedWords on real hardware): dest fixed at F64/Dd this cycle (the dominant + sqlite-blocking i64->double case); int->F32 (ftype=00, Sd) is FAIL-LOUD at the mir_to_lir SIToFP arm (a deferred narrow gap, D-CSUBSET-INT-TO-F32-CODEGEN). SCVTF Dd,Xn = 0x9E620000 = sf=1[0x80000000] | 11110[0x1E000000] | ftype=01[0x00400000] | bit21[0x00200000] | opcode=010[0x00020000]; SCVTF Dd,Wn = 0x1E620000 (same MINUS the sf bit). Cross-checked against the config's FCVTZS Dn base 0x9E780000 (opcode=000, rmode=11) — same field decode.

variants.$comment: SCVTF Dd, Xn — sf=1 64-bit signed source -> double. base 0x9E620000. The sqlite i64 -> volatile double blocker.
SCVTF Dd, Wn — sf=0 32-bit signed source -> double. base 0x1E620000 (the arm64 analog of cvtsi2sd xmm,r32; an I32 fits the F64 result exactly).

opcodes.$comment: D-CSUBSET-INT-FLOAT-CONVERSION (int→float, unsigned): UCVTF — Scalar Convert Unsigned integer to Floating-point. Same 'Conversion between FP and integer' group as SCVTF but opcode=011 [18:16] (the unsigned sibling). MIR UIToFP is the producer; the SOURCE-int-width axis matches si_to_fp (width=64 -> UCVTF Dd,Xn, width=32 -> UCVTF Dd,Wn). Dest fixed F64/Dd (U->F32 deferred D-CSUBSET-INT-TO-F32-CODEGEN). Unlike x86 SSE2 (which has no unsigned cvt and must fall back to cvtsi2sd), arm64 UCVTF handles the FULL unsigned i64 range natively — so the arm64 ui_to_fp is value-correct where x86's signed-cvt fallback is not (the deferred D-CSUBSET-UI-TO-FP-UNSIGNED-I64 is an x86-only gap; sqlite does not hit it on either target). UCVTF Dd,Xn = 0x9E630000 = SCVTF Dd,Xn[0x9E620000] | opcode bit [0x00010000]; UCVTF Dd,Wn = 0x1E630000.

variants.$comment: UCVTF Dd, Xn — sf=1 64-bit unsigned source -> double. base 0x9E630000.
UCVTF Dd, Wn — sf=0 32-bit unsigned source -> double. base 0x1E630000.

opcodes.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FMOV Dd, Dn — FP register-register move, double. Base 0x1E604000 = 0 0 0 11110 [31:24] | ftype=01 [23:22] | 1 [21] | opc=000000 FMOV [20:15] | 10000 [14:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rd [4:0] (FP data-processing 1 source — the single source rides Rn, NOT Rm; contrast the GPR mov's ORR alias whose source is Rm). Bound to the fpr class's `move` slot in registerClassOps[] — consumed by MIR value/phi copies and the callconv arg/return moves whenever the register class is FPR (the arm64 analog of x86's movaps role; a D-form copy moves the full 64-bit view, no upper-lane-merge hazard to dodge). FC3.5 sweep-c2 adjudication: register COPIES stay width-BLIND (variant deliberately width-absent, match-any) — the D-form move preserves an F32 value exactly (it lives in the low 4 bytes; S[n] is the low half of D[n]), the same reasoning as x86's full-register MOVAPS serving both widths. Only memory accesses (fldur/fstur) are width-exact.

D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FPR memory LOAD — LDUR Dt, [Xn, #simm9] (SIMD&FP 64-bit, unscaled). Fixed word 0xFC400000 = the GPR LDUR 0xF8400000 with V=1 (bit 26): size=11 [31:30] | 111 [29:27] | V=1 [26] | 00 [25:24] | opc=01 load [23:22] | 0 [21] | imm9 [20:12] | 00 [11:10] | Rn [9:5] | Rt [4:0]. Operand layout mirrors the GPR `load` row exactly (shared LIR load form [base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)] + result): result→Rt (rd), base→Rn, MemOffset→Imm9 (signed -256..255), MemBase carries no bits (membase.noscale). Bound to the fpr class's `load` slot in registerClassOps[] — the callconv epilogue saved-d-reg reload, stack-resident F64 args, and MIR Load of an F64 rodata global (the literal-materialization path) all resolve here. F64-only: the F32 S-form (size=10, 0xBC400000) lands with an F32 consumer.

encoding.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2 (D-CSUBSET-F32-CODEGEN): width-keyed pair — LDUR Dt (8-byte, 0xFC400000, width 64) vs LDUR St (4-byte, size=10 → 0xBC400000, width 32; ARM ARM C7.2 LDUR SIMD&FP). lowerLoad threads the loaded type's width for FPR-class results: an 8-byte D-load of a 4-byte F32 rodata item would read past the item.

variants.$comment: FC3.5: LDUR St, [Xn, #simm9] — size=10 single, base 0xBC400000 (4-byte load).

opcodes.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): FPR memory STORE — STUR Dt, [Xn, #simm9] (SIMD&FP 64-bit, unscaled). Fixed word 0xFC000000 = the GPR STUR 0xF8000000 with V=1 (bit 26); opc=00 store. Operand layout mirrors the GPR `store` row exactly (shared LIR store form [value_reg, base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)], no result): value→Rt (rd wire), base→Rn, MemOffset→Imm9 (signed). Bound to the fpr class's `store` slot in registerClassOps[] — the callconv prologue saved-d-reg spill is the first consumer (AAPCS64 declares d8-d15 callee-saved + regalloc prefers callee-saved, so the FIRST FPR vreg in any function needs this store — the exact shape that surfaced x86's movsd_store on ms_x64).

encoding.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: width-keyed pair — STUR Dt (8-byte, width 64) vs STUR St (4-byte, 0xBC000000, width 32). lowerStore threads the stored VALUE's type width for FPR-class stores; the regalloc spill path stays width-default 64 over 8-byte slots (value-preserving for both float widths).

variants.$comment: FC3.5: STUR St, [Xn, #simm9] — size=10 single, base 0xBC000000 (4-byte store).

opcodes.$comment: FC12c (D-FC12C-AAPCS64-VARIADIC-CALLEE, BLOCKER-1): FPR 128-bit memory STORE — STUR Qt, [Xn, #simm9] (SIMD&FP 128-bit, unscaled). Used to spill the v0..v7 VR arg registers (16-byte slots) into the AAPCS64 variadic register-save-area's VR block so va_arg(double) reads the right 8 bytes. CRITICAL fixedWord = 0x3C800000 = 1015021568, NOT 0x3C000000: the latter is STURB (8-bit byte store) and would spill one byte per FP slot → va_arg(double) garbage. Derivation (ARM ARM C7 LDR/STR SIMD&FP unscaled, 128-bit): Q form size[31:30]=00, [29:27]=111, V[26]=1, opc[23:22]=10 (store, 128-bit) → 0x00000000(size=00) | 0x38000000([29:27]=111) | 0x04000000(V=1,[26]) | 0x00800000(opc=10,[23:22]) = 0x3C800000. Operand layout is IDENTICAL to the D/S-form fstur rows above (shared LIR store form [value_reg, base_reg, MemBase(scale=1), MemOffset(disp)], no result): value→Rt (rd wire), base→Rn, MemOffset→Imm9 (signed -256..255). Single 128-bit form (no width variants — Q is always 16 bytes). Byte-pin: STUR Q0,[X1,#0] = 0x3C800000 (Rd=0,Rn=1<<5=0x20,Imm9=0 → 0x3C800020), LE bytes 20 00 80 3C.

$condCodeEncodingComment: D-AS3-COND-CODE-ARM64 (mirror of x86's table): per-cond-code nibble for the AArch64 B.cond instruction's bits [3:0]. Numeric values are the AArch64 condition codes (ARM ARM C1.2.4): EQ=0, NE=1, CS/HS=2, CC/LO=3, HI=8, LS=9, GE=10(0xA), LT=11(0xB), GT=12(0xC), LE=13(0xD). Signed comparisons map slt→LT(0xB)=11, sle→LE(0xD)=13, sgt→GT(0xC)=12, sge→GE(0xA)=10; unsigned map ult→CC/LO=3, ule→LS=9, ugt→HI=8, uge→CS/HS=2. The encoder ORs this nibble into word 0 bits 0..3 of the jcc macro (vs x86's low 4 bits of the opcode byte — same field-position abstraction, different ISA placement). The 10 integer arms are REQUIRED; the FC3.5 float arms (next comment) are per-entry optional.
$condCodeEncodingFloatComment: FC3.5 sweep-c2 (FCmp lowering — D-COND-FLOAT-NAN-TRUTHINESS-FCMP): float condition arms over the FCMP NZCV truth table (ARM ARM: equal → Z=1,C=1; less → N=1; greater → C=1; unordered → C=1,V=1). fogt = GT (Z=0∧N=V, 0xC — unordered has N=0≠V=1 → false ✓; NOTE the integer-ugt HI=8 would be TRUE on unordered, a NaN miscompile — this is why float codes are separate); foge = GE (N=V, 0xA); foeq = EQ (Z=1, 0 — unordered Z=0 → false ✓); fune = NE (Z=0, 1 — TRUE on unordered, exactly C 6.5.9 `!=`); fuo = VS (V=1, 6 — after FCMP the overflow flag IS the unordered predicate); ford = VC (7). fone is deliberately ABSENT: NE is true on unordered, and no single AArch64 cond is `ordered ∧ unequal` — its absence signals the composed realization (One = cset-NE ∧ cset-VC), the mirror of x86's foeq/fune absence. Olt/Ole need no entries — the lowering swap-canonicalizes them to fogt/foge (MI/LS would also realize them directly; the swap keeps ONE target-blind rule).
$registersComment: AAPCS64 general-purpose registers x0..x30 + zero-register XZR (encoded as 31 in source operands; uses register's hwEncoding). Stack pointer `sp` is also encoded as 31 but in the SP-slot context — hwEncoding=31 with class GPR is shared between XZR and SP at the byte level. D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): the SIMD&FP file is declared as d0..d31 (class fpr) — the V registers' 64-bit D views, sharing the same 5-bit field encoding.

registers.$comment: Stack pointer shares hwEncoding=31 with XZR at the byte level (the context determines which: SP-using addressing modes vs ZR-encoding instructions). The substrate treats them as distinct names with distinct ordinals; the JSON-side ordinal is what matters for the cc lookup.
registers.name: sp

registers.$comment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): the V-register file's 64-bit D views, class fpr. FC3.5 sweep-c2: F32 values live in the S views (the low 32 bits of the same registers) — the width axis on the instruction VARIANTS selects D vs S forms; the register declarations stay the D views (the 5-bit hwEncoding is shared by every view, and copies/spills move the containing D view, value-preserving for F32). widthBytes=8 is the D view, NOT the full 128-bit V register — the q/v views (and their subOf aliasing) are the future F128/SIMD cycle's work. hwEncoding = the 5-bit register number every SIMD&FP instruction form shares.
registers.name: d0

$registerClassOpsComment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): per-register-class move/load/store mnemonics — the same FC2 Part B table x86_64 declares. A class WITHOUT a row uses the universal defaults (mov/load/store) iff it is gpr — the substrate default class; every other row-less class FAILS LOUD at the consumer (L_RequiredLirOpcodeMissing), because the class-blind alternative is a silent miscompile (a GPR ORR/LDUR against a D-register hwEncoding assembles to valid-looking words that move the WRONG register file). fpr: move=FMOV(D), load=LDUR(D), store=STUR(D).
$callingConventionsComment: AAPCS64 (https://github.com/ARM-software/abi-aa/blob/main/aapcs64/aapcs64.rst). Integer args: x0..x7. FP args: d0..d7 (D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE 2026-06-10 — declared with the FPR substrate). Caller-saved: x0..x18 (incl. x16/x17 = IP0/IP1; PLT-clobberable) + v0-v7/v16-v31 in their d views. Callee-saved: x19..x28 + x29 (frame ptr) + x30 (link reg) + v8-v15 low 64 bits (d8..d15).

callingConventions.name: aapcs64
callingConventions.$aggregateComment: FC7 C3 (D-FC7-STRUCT-BY-VALUE-ARG-RETURN): AAPCS64 §5.4 by-value aggregates. HFA (1-4 leaves all the same FP type) → that many FPR pieces (v0..v3 / d0..d3 for returns, d0..d7 for args). A non-HFA aggregate <=16 bytes → 1-2 GPR pieces (x0..x1 returns / x0..x7 args); > 16 bytes → BY REFERENCE: a caller-allocated copy for args; for RETURNS the caller passes the result address in x8 (`indirectResultRegister`) and the callee returns VOID (it writes through x8 — distinct from SysV/Win64's hidden-arg-then-return-pointer; `aggregateSretViaHiddenArg` is false here because indirectResultRegister is set). `aapcs64_hfa` drives the bounded classifier arm; maxRegBytes = 16 (two X-registers).
callingConventions.$aggregateStackExhaustsComment: D-FC12-VARIADIC-OVERFLOW-FIXED-AGGREGATE-STACK-ARGS: AAPCS64 §6.4.2 stage C — when a by-value composite/HFA does not fit the remaining arg registers it is placed WHOLLY on the stack and the overflowed class is EXHAUSTED (gcc aarch64_layout_arg sets aapcs_nextncrn=8 / aapcs_nextnvrn=8), so every subsequent arg of that class also goes to memory. true = no backfill (contrast SysV, which backfills the leftover registers). Read by HIR→MIR's caller+callee cursor handling + va_start's __gr_offs/__vr_offs clamp.
callingConventions.$callerSavedFprComment: D-ARM64-FLOAT-SUBSTRATE (2026-06-10): AAPCS64 §6.1.2 — v0-v7 (args/results) and v16-v31 are caller-saved; declared here in their d views.
